sections.context: src/linter/core.ts is a kitchen sink that loads config, runs markdownlint, runs CLI linters, discovers project roots, parses linter output, builds code excerpts, and merges outcomes. Its interface is nearly as large as its implementation, so callers and tests must reach into many exported helpers. This refactor introduces a LinterPipeline module with a small, deep interface and hides the linter adapters, config loading, report parsing, and outcome merging behind it.
sections.behavior_invariant: Given the same cwd, loaded LinterConfig, and set of modified file paths, the system must produce the same ValidationOutcome kind (clean/findings/tool-error), the same normalized affectedFiles list, the same reportMode, and the same bounded summary message content and sidecar metadata shape as before the refactor. The /post-turn-linter-run, /post-turn-linter-fix, and /post-turn-linter-report commands must continue to behave identically for users. Existing test assertions that are not tied to internal helper names must remain valid or be replaced by equivalent pipeline-seam tests.
sections.approach: Use a monolithic LinterPipeline with a public surface of runChecks(files) -> ValidationOutcome, summarize(outcome, reportId) -> summary, and persist(report) -> sidecar. Adapters are internal by default; a test-only constructor accepts fake adapters so tests drive the seam instead of calling __test__ internals. Split src/linter/core.ts into: src/linter/pipeline.ts (orchestrator), src/linter/config-loader.ts, src/linter/adapters/markdownlint.ts, src/linter/adapters/cli.ts, src/linter/adapters/lsp.ts (refactored from existing lsp.ts), src/linter/report-builder.ts (parse + excerpts), src/linter/outcome-merger.ts. Keep src/linter/index.ts as a thin extension orchestrator that owns state and commands. Rejected alternatives: (1) explicit dependency-injection pipeline — larger interface, leaks adapter composition; (2) three separate seams (runner/formatter/store) — more flexible but shallower and forces the orchestrator to know sequence; (3) adapter-by-adapter migration — rejected in favor of the full-pipeline PR Dave selected.
sections.scope: In scope: src/linter/core.ts split, src/linter/index.ts migration to the pipeline seam, src/linter/lsp.ts adapter refactor, src/linter/report-hygiene.ts summary builder kept as a pipeline consumer, and test/index.test.ts updated to use the seam. Out of scope: shared sidecar store with the reviewer (Candidate 2), reviewer context seams (Candidate 3), LSP client/server internals, adding new linter types, and README/feature changes beyond reflecting new module layout.
sections.characterization_tests: Before moving code, add tests in test/linter-pipeline.test.ts that exercise runQueuedLintChecks through the current public surface with in-memory file fixtures and stubbed spawn. Capture: markdownlint API path, CLI per-file grouping, project-root/workspace CLI modes, LSP merge behavior, and code excerpt generation. These tests pin behavior and must still pass after the refactor.

name: Deepen linter run-checks pipeline
outcomeNote: Implemented LinterPipeline seam, migrated src/linter/index.ts, extracted adapters/config/merger/report modules, deleted obsolete lsp.ts, updated tests, and opened PR #2. TypeScript, Biome, and Vitest all pass.

sections.context: The post-turn-linter and post-turn-reviewer both persist redacted reports to sidecar files and recover them via preview/slice/full modes. Today that logic is duplicated across src/linter/report-hygiene.ts (~631 lines) and src/reviewer/report-hygiene.ts (~343 lines). Redaction rules, path sanitization, SHA-256/byte accounting, temp-file writes, and recovery policy are implemented twice, so any security or storage fix has to be made in two places. This refactor moves the shared storage contract into one module and leaves each subsystem responsible only for its summary formatting and command argument parsing.
sections.behavior_invariant: After the refactor: (1) sidecar files are written atomically via temp-file + rename to the same ~/.pi/agent/tool-output/<sessionId>/<uuid>.json path; (2) persisted JSON contains the same metadata fields including id, toolName, sessionId, path, createdAt, originalChars, originalBytes, redactedChars, redactedBytes, originalSha256, redactedSha256, summaryMode, and optional failureState; (3) redaction removes the same secret patterns (api keys, bearer tokens, AWS keys, JWTs); (4) preview, slice, metadata, and full recovery modes behave identically, including the requirement that full recovery requires --ack-context-cost unless allowFullWithoutAck is true; (5) the default sidecar directory derivation and session-id derivation remain unchanged; (6) existing callers importing writeLinterReportSidecar / recoverLinterReportSidecar / writeReviewerReportSidecar / recoverReviewerReportSidecar continue to compile and behave the same.
sections.approach: Introduce a minimal generic core at src/shared/report-sidecar.ts that exposes writeReportSidecar<M extends ReportSidecarMetadata> and recoverReportSidecar<M extends ReportSidecarMetadata>. Callers pass toolName and summaryMode per invocation, so the core stays stateless and the existing linter/reviewer metadata types remain typed. Move isQualityGatesSubAgentRuntime to a tiny src/shared/runtime-detection.ts because it is currently duplicated and is used to decide recovery policy. Reduce src/linter/report-hygiene.ts and src/reviewer/report-hygiene.ts to summary formatting, argument parsing, and thin re-exports/wrappers that delegate storage to the shared module. This follows the existing Mulch pattern of preferring a deep seam (generic sidecar storage) over shallow helper modules and keeping adapter identity and file routing explicit.

name: Unify report sidecar module
outcomeNote: Shared report-sidecar seam extracted; linter/reviewer wrappers delegate storage/redaction/recovery. All tests, typecheck, and build pass. PR #3 opened.

sections.context: src/reviewer/index.ts is an 841-line extension entry point that combines Pi lifecycle hooks, a state machine, command registration, report formatting, reviewer sidecar persistence, and fix-request scheduling. After the LinterPipeline refactor, the linter extension has a clear run-checks seam, but the reviewer extension still exposes its internal state shape and transition rules to callers and tests. This refactor introduces a ReviewerOrchestrator module with a small, event-driven interface and hides the state machine, formatting, sidecar writes, and scheduling behind it.
sections.behavior_invariant: Given the same session events and linter status messages, the reviewer must produce the same phase transitions, the same custom_message outputs (advisory, findings, escalation, timeout, parse-fail, report), the same fix-up user messages, the same sidecar metadata shape, and the same /reviewer-* command behavior as before the refactor. Default config and CLI invocation paths must remain unchanged. Existing tests that assert behavior (not internal helper names) must stay valid or be replaced by equivalent orchestrator-seam tests.
sections.approach: Introduce src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts with an event-driven interface: initialize(ctx), onLinterClean(files, timestamp?), onTurnEnd(ctx), requestReview(ctx, opts?), registerCommands(pi), and getStateSnapshot(). The orchestrator owns the state machine, config/skip loading, report formatting, sidecar persistence, and fix-request scheduling. src/reviewer/index.ts becomes thin Pi wiring that delegates events and commands to the orchestrator. Tests drive the seam by injecting fake runReview, writeSidecar, loadConfig, and loadSkipFilter dependencies. Rejected alternatives: (1) explicit lifecycle + command dispatch — more uniform but forces the extension to map every Pi command name; (2) dependency-injected controller returning command handlers — slightly larger interface and leaks command wiring details; (3) also deepening the reviewer core pipeline in the same PR — too large; kept as a follow-up candidate.
sections.scope: In scope: splitting state machine + lifecycle logic out of src/reviewer/index.ts into src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts, defining a small orchestrator interface, migrating Pi event/command wiring, adding characterization tests, updating tests to use the new seam, and removing now-redundant __test__ exports from src/reviewer/index.ts. Out of scope: refactoring src/reviewer/reviewer.ts into a ReviewPipeline (Candidate 3), linter controller refactor (Candidate 2), shared change-detection module (Candidate 4), new reviewer domains or prompts, and changes to the shared sidecar store.

name: Deepen reviewer extension entry point
outcomeNote: Implemented ReviewerOrchestrator seam, migrated src/reviewer/index.ts to thin wiring, added orchestrator tests, completed self-review pass, and opened PR #4. All validation passes.

execution_profile.parallel_subagents: allowed for independent subtasks only; initial dependency graph is mostly sequential

sections.context: src/reviewer/reviewer.ts is still a shallow toolbox module after the ReviewerOrchestrator refactor. It exposes gatherDiff, applyDiffFilters, renderTaskTemplate, spawnReviewer, parseReviewReport, hasFindingsAboveThreshold, and display helpers, so callers must know the execution order and failure modes for diff gathering, prompt rendering, child Pi execution, output parsing, and normalized review result handling. The selected candidate deepens this into a reviewer execution seam consumed by the orchestrator, while preserving the orchestrator as owner of session state, re-review loops, notifications, sidecar writes, and fix-turn scheduling.
sections.behavior_invariant: Given the same task, files, cwd, ReviewConfig, skip/gitignore filter options, prompts, child process output, stderr, exit code, timeout status, and abort signal, the refactored reviewer execution path must return the same ReviewerResult shape, parsed ReviewReport, rawOutput/stderr handling, sanitized command string, timeout behavior, diff truncation behavior, prompt rendering content, and file filtering behavior as the current helper sequence. Existing /reviewer-* command behavior, ReviewerOrchestrator phase transitions, sidecar metadata shape, parse-fail notices, timeout notices, advisory/fix/escalation messages, and default config behavior must remain unchanged.
sections.approach: Use the selected minimal reviewer execution seam: ReviewerExecution.runAttempt(input) -> Promise<ReviewerResult>, where input contains task, files, cwd, ReviewConfig, DiffFilterOptions, and optional AbortSignal. This follows the repo pattern of a deep pipeline/orchestrator seam with implementation details hidden behind it. The execution module owns context gathering for one review attempt: apply diff filters, gather/cap diff, read/render prompts, spawn the child Pi reviewer, parse output, and return the normalized ReviewerResult. src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts keeps the state machine and calls the seam; src/reviewer/index.ts only wires the default execution adapter. Rejected alternatives: (1) Pipeline object with gather/render/execute methods, rejected because it still exposes sequencing to callers; (2) explicit ports-and-adapters interface, rejected as over-designed until there are multiple real diff/prompt/child adapters; (3) changing prompt content or review domains, rejected as out of scope.
sections.chosen_seam: ReviewerExecution.runAttempt(input) -> ReviewerResult. The seam lives near src/reviewer/reviewer.ts or a new src/reviewer/execution.ts. It represents one review attempt, not the whole reviewer state machine.
sections.scope: In scope: characterization tests for current reviewer execution behavior, a reviewer execution interface/type, implementation of runAttempt using the existing helper logic, migration of ReviewerOrchestrator/default wiring to call the seam, tests that cross the same seam as callers, and cleanup of now-redundant public helper exports only when safe. Out of scope: prompt content changes, review domain changes, sidecar storage format changes, linter controller refactor, LSP diagnostics refactor, and changes to re-review policy beyond preserving existing behavior.

name: Deepen reviewer execution seam

execution_profile.parallel_subagents: allowed for independent review/validation or low-coupling test tasks

sections.context: src/linter/index.ts is an 853-line extension entry point that combines Pi lifecycle hooks, modified-file detection, pending-tool state, clean-file cache, cooldown/dedupe state, LSP config reconstruction, linter execution, sidecar recovery, status reporting, command handlers, and fix-turn scheduling. The LinterPipeline already provides a deep run-checks seam, but the extension controller behavior is still mixed with Pi wiring. This refactor mirrors the validated ReviewerOrchestrator pattern: move controller behavior behind a linter event orchestrator seam and leave src/linter/index.ts as thin wiring.
sections.behavior_invariant: Given the same session events, tool execution events, command invocations, cwd, LinterConfig, LspDiagnosticsConfig, sidecar metadata, modified file paths, file stats, pipeline outcomes, and sub-agent runtime mode, the refactored linter controller must emit the same custom messages/status messages, notifications, fix-turn prompts, report sidecar recovery behavior, queued-file behavior, cooldown behavior, dedupe behavior, clean-file cache behavior, LSP config persistence/reconstruction, and /post-turn-linter-* command behavior as before. LinterPipeline semantics, adapter behavior, report sidecar format, and default config behavior must remain unchanged.
sections.approach: Use the selected event orchestrator seam: LinterOrchestrator with initialize(ctx), shutdown(ctx), onSessionTree(ctx), onToolExecutionStart(event), onToolExecutionEnd(event, ctx), onTurnEnd(ctx), registerCommands(pi), and getStateSnapshot(). The orchestrator owns lifecycle state, file queueing, cooldown, dedupe, latest report, sidecar recovery, and fix-turn policy. src/linter/index.ts becomes thin Pi wiring that creates the orchestrator and forwards Pi events, symmetric with src/reviewer/index.ts. LinterPipeline remains the execution dependency. Rejected alternatives: (1) command-only controller, rejected because event state and commands share latest report/fix-turn state; (2) file-queue-only module, rejected because it leaves the shallow extension entry point mostly unchanged.
sections.chosen_seam: LinterOrchestrator event seam in src/linter/orchestrator.ts. It represents extension controller behavior, not linter execution itself. The execution module remains LinterPipeline.
sections.scope: In scope: introduce src/linter/orchestrator.ts, move controller state and helper functions from src/linter/index.ts, thin src/linter/index.ts to Pi wiring, update tests to import/use the orchestrator seam, preserve test-only helper access where needed, and validate behavior. Out of scope: changing linter adapter behavior, changing LinterPipeline semantics, changing report sidecar storage format, LSP diagnostics refactor, reviewer refactors, and removing the legacy src/linter/core.ts facade.

name: Deepen linter extension controller seam
outcomeNote: Implemented LinterOrchestrator event seam, thinned src/linter/index.ts to Pi wiring, preserved LinterPipeline/report/sidecar/fix-turn behavior, passed biome/typecheck/build typecheck/vitest, completed read-only review, and opened PR https://github.com/davehardy20/pi-quality-gates/pull/6.

sections.context: The post-turn-reviewer auto-trigger (src/reviewer/{index,orchestrator,config,commands,report-hygiene}.ts + prompts) never worked as Dave intended. The pr-gate subsystem (/pr-review command + push gate) is now the chosen review path and reuses only the reusable execution core (reviewer.ts, types.ts, reviewer-skip.ts). Retiring the auto state machine removes ~1,951 LOC of dead/failing behaviour, drops 4 commands (/reviewer-status|run|model|toggle), and eliminates the confusing duplication of two review paths in one bundle. The keep-layer is verified to have zero dependency on the retiring layer, so the removal is safe and mechanical. Relocating the keep-layer into pr-gate/ is explicitly out of scope (deferred candidate).
sections.behavior_invariant: pr-gate's /pr-review path keeps building and its tests stay green because it imports createReviewerExecution/formatReportForDisplay from reviewer/reviewer.ts, loadSkipFilter from reviewer/reviewer-skip.ts, and ReviewConfig from reviewer/types.ts — all three kept unchanged in place. The post-turn linter is completely unaffected (it shares only src/shared modules that remain in use). The auto turn_end review trigger and the four /reviewer-* commands are entirely removed from the extension surface. No src/shared module becomes an orphan (linter still consumes report-sidecar, review-scope, review-severity, review-types). build/typecheck/vitest all pass.
sections.approach: Pure deletion, leave the keep-layer in place. Delete the 7 retiring files (5 .ts + 2 prompts .md), remove the postTurnReviewerExtension import and call from src/index.ts (2 lines), delete test/reviewer-orchestrator.test.ts wholesale, and surgically remove the orchestrator __test__ import plus all report-hygiene test blocks and imports from test/index.test.ts while preserving any reviewer.ts/reviewer-skip blocks still exercised by pr-gate. Update README to drop the Post-Turn Reviewer section, workflow diagram, reviewer config block, and the /reviewer-* command list. No relocation, no shared/ promotion — those are deferred. Follows the deletion test: removing these files removes real dead complexity without forcing it to reappear across callers (pr-gate already has its own equivalents). Aligns with mx-46fdf3's ReviewerExecution-half (kept) while voiding its ReviewerOrchestrator-half (retired).
